title: Phaeocephalum distans
taxonomy: Phaeocephalum distans is classified within the family Parmeliaceae, which is a group of lichen-forming fungi. This classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. The genus Phaeocephalum is a part of the order Lecanorales. The species P. distans is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions of the world. It is a type of crustose lichen, which is characterized by a crust-like growth habit on the substrate.
uses: Phaeocephalum distans has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. However, the plant's medicinal uses are not extensively studied and more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
